methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuamCore is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alon Cohen that has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 11 peers across quantum computing—three public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion—though stage-specific peer multiples proved insufficient for robust triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using the $35M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $70M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables; while seven same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier classification. Relying on all-sector median multiples would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models and maturity profiles.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $60M (low) to $80M (high), with a median point of $70M. This relatively narrow band reflects dependence on a single method. Key uncertainties center on stage-progression assumptions—the 2.0x multiplier may not hold if quantum computing capital efficiency or investor appetite diverges from broader venture patterns—and on QuamCore's specific technical differentiation relative to the peer set, which this analysis does not assess.
